light-and-bright.name: Light & Bright
light-and-bright.title: Light & Bright — MT. PEAK · Himalayan Black Tea
light-and-bright.metaDesc: Light & Bright — a partially oxidised single-origin black tea from eastern Nepal. Floral, bright, golden cup. Certified organic, hand-plucked.
light-and-bright.desc: A black tea of unusual restraint. Oxidised only partially, it keeps the brightness of the high spring garden — a golden cup with a floral lift and a clean, luminous finish that lingers like morning light on the ridge.
light-and-bright.homeDesc: A black tea of unusual restraint. Oxidised only partially, it keeps the brightness of the high spring garden — a clean, luminous finish that lingers like morning light on the ridge.
light-and-bright.stock: In stock · ships within 2 working days

gallery.label: DRY LEAF — MACRO
gallery.sub: Twisted black leaf with golden tips, shot like jewellery

gallery.label: THE LIQUOR — BRIGHT GOLD
gallery.sub: The brewed cup, backlit so the golden colour glows

tasting.v: Orchard blossom, apricot skin, a thread of mountain honey.
Light-bodied and luminous — floral lift over a soft, golden sweetness.
Clean and lingering, like morning light holding on the ridge.

brewSteps: Warm a porcelain or glass pot with hot water, then discard — the vessel should be ready, not scalding.
Measure three grams of leaf per 200ml. This is a tea that rewards a generous, unhurried hand.
Pour water just off the boil, around 90°C — hot enough to open the leaf, cool enough to keep it bright.
Steep three minutes for the first cup. Re-infuse up to three times, adding thirty seconds each pour.

make.d: Two leaves and a bud, taken by hand at first light.
12–14 hours on bamboo trays, until the leaf turns supple.
Gentle pressure, bruising the leaf only lightly.
Arrested early, at 20–30% — the secret to its brightness.
Low and slow, locking the luminous character in.

light-and-bright.originLead: High on the southern slopes of the Himalaya, above the cloud line, the garden grows slowly in thin, clean air.
originPs: Light & Bright is picked in the first weeks of spring, when the leaf carries the brightness of the new season. Cold nights and altitude force it to grow slowly — concentrating aroma, sharpening flavour, and giving the cup its luminous, floral lift.

Every leaf is certified organic, plucked by hands that have read these slopes for generations, and finished at our own factory. No middlemen. Only the mountain, the leaf, and the people who tend both.

spec.v: Two leaves and a bud · by hand

light-and-bright.closerP: £68 for 100g of single-origin, hand-plucked Himalayan black tea — drawn bright, finished clean.

mountain-green.name: Mountain Green
mountain-green.title: Mountain Green — MT. PEAK · Himalayan Green Tea
mountain-green.metaDesc: Mountain Green — a pan-fired single-origin green tea from eastern Nepal. Fresh, vegetal, crisp, pale jade cup. Certified organic, hand-plucked.
mountain-green.desc: Pan-fired within hours of plucking, the leaf never oxidises. What reaches the cup is the mountain itself — fresh, vegetal, crisp as thin air, with a sweetness drawn from above the cloud line.
mountain-green.homeDesc: Pan-fired within hours of plucking, the leaf never oxidises. What reaches the cup is the mountain itself — fresh, vegetal, crisp as thin air, with a sweetness drawn from above the cloud line.
mountain-green.stock: In stock · ships within 2 working days
mountain-green.heroAlt: Mt. Peak Mountain Green — 100g loose-leaf caddy

gallery.label: DRY LEAF — MACRO
gallery.sub: Whole pan-fired leaf, still faintly downy — shot like jewellery

gallery.label: THE LIQUOR — PALE JADE
gallery.sub: The brewed cup, backlit so the pale jade colour glows

meta.v: Spring flush, hand-picked and fired same day

mountain-green.tastingH: A pale jade cup, crisp as altitude

tasting.v: Fresh-cut grass, steamed edamame, a cool mineral note.
Crisp and vegetal, light-bodied, with a clean sweetness underneath.
Short and refreshing — the taste of thin, cold mountain air.

brewSteps: Warm the vessel briefly — green tea is unforgiving of heat, so keep it gentle throughout.
Measure two grams of leaf per 200ml. Less is more with a leaf this delicate.
Cool the water well below boiling, to around 75°C, to avoid scorching the leaf and turning it bitter.
Steep two minutes for the first cup. A second, shorter infusion is often the sweetest.

make.d: Two leaves and a bud, taken by hand at first light.
A brief rest, just long enough to soften the leaf.
Fired within hours of plucking, halting oxidation entirely.
Low, slow heat locks in the fresh, vegetal character.

mountain-green.originLead: Above the cloud line, where the air thins and the nights turn cold, the leaf grows slowly and stays green.
originPs: Mountain Green is fired within hours of plucking — no oxidation, no waiting. What reaches the cup is the closest thing to the living leaf: fresh, crisp, and faintly sweet, the way the garden tastes at first light.

Every leaf is certified organic, plucked by hands that have read these slopes for generations, and finished at our own factory. No middlemen. Only the mountain, the leaf, and the people who tend both.

spec.v: Two leaves and a bud · by hand

mountain-green.closerP: £74 for 100g of single-origin, hand-plucked, pan-fired Himalayan green tea — crisp, fresh, unmistakably alive.

golden-harvest.name: Golden Harvest
golden-harvest.title: Golden Harvest — MT. PEAK · Himalayan Golden Tips Gift Box
golden-harvest.metaDesc: Golden Harvest — golden-tip Himalayan black tea, hand-finished gift box. Honey-sweet, round, fully oxidised. Single-origin, organic, from eastern Nepal.
golden-harvest.desc: Golden tips, selected one by one and oxidised under close watch — round, honey-sweet, burnished with apricot and warm spice. Presented in the hand-finished gift box, made to be given.
golden-harvest.homeDesc: Golden tips, selected one by one and oxidised under close watch — round, honey-sweet, burnished with apricot and warm spice. Presented in the hand-finished gift box, made to be given.
golden-harvest.stock: In stock · ships within 2 working days
golden-harvest.heroAlt: Mt. Peak Golden Harvest — gift box · 100g

gallery.label: DRY LEAF — MACRO
gallery.sub: Golden tips, selected one by one, shot like jewellery

gallery.label: THE LIQUOR — DEEP AMBER
gallery.sub: The brewed cup, backlit so the deep amber colour glows

meta.v: Peak season golden-tip selection, by hand

golden-harvest.tastingH: A deep amber cup, honeyed and round

tasting.v: Dried apricot, warm spice, a deep note of wildflower honey.
Full and round, honey-sweet, with a smooth, malty depth.
Long, warm and lingering — like the last light before dusk.

brewSteps: Warm the pot properly — this leaf is dense and gives its best at full temperature.
Measure four grams of leaf per 200ml. A generous hand rewards you here.
Use water close to a full boil, around 95°C, to draw out the depth and sweetness fully.
Steep four minutes for the first cup. A second infusion, slightly longer, is rich and rewarding.

make.d: Golden tips chosen one by one at peak season.
A full day on bamboo trays, until the leaf turns supple.
Rolled fully, to open the leaf for complete oxidation.
Carried through fully, under close watch, for depth and colour.
Finished slowly, then hand-packed into the gift box.

golden-harvest.originLead: At the peak of the season, when the tips turn gold, our pickers choose only the very best of the garden.
originPs: Golden Harvest is our most generous tea — fully oxidised, deep in colour, honeyed in the cup. Every tip is selected by hand at the height of the season, then carried through a full, careful oxidation to bring out its warmth and depth.

Presented in a hand-finished gift box, it is built to be given as much as brewed. Certified organic, single-origin, and finished at our own factory — no middlemen, only the mountain and the hands that tend it.

spec.v: Fully oxidised black tea · golden tips

golden-harvest.closerP: £115 for the hand-finished gift box — 100g of golden-tip Himalayan black tea, honeyed and full.
